@holistic.voicework

Know your
voice
to know
yourself.

You already carry the most advanced technology there is. It lives in your body, and it speaks through your voice — body, breath, sound. Older than any therapy. Hidden in plain sight. And entirely yours to reclaim.

Get the free worksheet Learn more ↓

Your voice was free once. Then life happened — someone laughed, someone corrected you, someone told you to lower it or raise it or change it. And slowly, without deciding to, you started holding back.

The voice is a mirror. The places where it tightens, disappears, or strains are rarely just technical — they reflect something deeper. When we allow emotions to be acknowledged — not fixed, not bypassed, just met — the musculature releases. The breath drops. The sound opens. To know your voice is, inevitably, to know yourself.

"What you're looking for is not just a technique. It's the thread back to yourself."

What clients say
"I was looking for a singing teacher who was also trauma-informed, gentle and reassuring — and Gabriela turned out to be all of that. The initial singing classes turned soon into some sort of sound therapy session — one of the most profound and impactful forms of therapy I've experienced so far. Gabriela holds the space in an amazing way, is very careful not to overwhelm me, and picks up subtle cues in me, even ones I didn't notice. I hope for everyone to experience this gift of healing that Gabriela brings to the world."
Lena  —  Berlin, Germany

I'm Gabriela.

I am an artist, first and always. I grew up in Argentina, the daughter of a neuroscientist and a psychologist — curiosity about how humans work has always been in my bones. When I was twelve, I lost my mother. And without fully understanding why, I began to sing.

I started as a jazz singer. It took me further than I expected — touring Argentina, Europe and Japan, earning recognition as one of the country's outstanding jazz voices, and landing on the Brazilian Netflix series Coisa Mais Linda. My second album marked a turning point: original songs about becoming, rooted in Argentine folklore and trip hop. As an actress, I trained at the Art of Acting Studio (Stella Adler) in Hollywood, and appeared in films that screened at BAFICI, Mar del Plata, and Marché du Film in Cannes — and most recently on stage at Theater Gorki in Berlin. I hold a degree in Voice, Speech, Language and Hearing Therapy, and have been guiding voice work with an integrative approach for over fifteen years.

In 2023, a PTSD diagnosis gave me clarity: I had been unconsciously regulating my nervous system through singing for years. As a voice therapist I already knew the vagus nerve governs both the vocal cords and the diaphragm — Polyvagal theory confirmed what I had felt in my own body, and witnessed in my students all along.

Art gave me freedom. Now I train people in theirs.

More voices
"One of the most valuable things about Gabriela's approach is that she does so from a place of great honesty and depth. She values listening attentively to the desires and needs of each student, accompanying them in the construction of their identity."
Julieta  —  Argentina
"The main thing was learning to breathe better, which gave me more air and energy. My voice, which had previously been stuck inside, was freed up. I found myself more confident, more expressive, and more aware of my body and what I transmit."
Vitor  —  Argentina
"I couldn't find my voice — not only in a physical way, but also spiritually. It was as if something had always been stuck in my throat. Gabriela's delicate, loving work has helped me free the sounds in my body and has given them back to me."
Arantxa  —  Spain
